question: Did the model select Option B as the correct statement?
Did the model determine that Option A is incorrect (i.e., the wall's normal force does not remain unchanged)?
Did the model determine that Option C is incorrect (i.e., the ground normal force on the block does not increase)?
Did the model determine that Option D is incorrect (i.e., the pushing force F does not decrease)?
Does the model clearly state a single correct option as the final answer?
Did the model correctly identify that all contact surfaces are frictionless (smooth)?
Does the reasoning correctly treat the process as quasi-static (system remains in static equilibrium at each step)?
Does the model correctly identify the three forces acting on sphere A: Gravity (down), Wall Normal (horizontal), and Block Normal (perpendicular to surface)?
Did the model use a valid method (e.g., vector triangle, component decomposition) to analyze the equilibrium of sphere A?
Did the model correctly reason that as block B moves right, the contact point shifts such that the angle of the block's normal force with the vertical increases?
Did the model correctly conclude that the block's normal force on the sphere must increase to balance the constant gravity as the angle increases?
Did the model correctly conclude that the wall's normal force on the sphere must increase as the angle changes?
Did the model correctly analyze the vertical equilibrium of the whole system to conclude that the ground support force remains constant?
Did the model correctly analyze the horizontal equilibrium of the system to conclude that the pushing force F balances the wall's normal force (F = N1)?
Did the model correctly conclude that the pushing force F increases because the wall's normal force increases?
Does the reasoning avoid introducing incorrect forces (e.g., friction, spurious torques) or invalid energy arguments?
Does the generated image include a free-body diagram (FBD) specifically for sphere A?
In the FBD for sphere A, is the gravity vector drawn pointing vertically downward?
In the FBD for sphere A, is the wall normal force drawn pointing horizontally to the left (away from the wall)?
In the FBD for sphere A, is the block normal force drawn perpendicular to the curved contact surface (pointing generally upward/right)?
Does the generated image correctly depict the system configuration with sphere A sandwiched between a vertical wall and a curved block B?
Is the pushing force F on block B shown as a horizontal vector?
Is the contact point between the sphere and block shown on the curved surface of the block?
Are the force vectors in the generated images clearly distinguishable (visible arrowheads, distinct from background)?
Are the text labels for forces (e.g., G, N1, N2, F) legible and placed near their respective vectors?
Do the force labels used in the text match the labels in the generated images?
Does the generated image visually reflect the geometric relationships described in the text (e.g., vector triangle or specific angles)?
Is there any contradiction between the text's description of force directions and the vectors shown in the images?
Are there any contradictions regarding friction (e.g., text says frictionless but image shows friction vectors)?
